provenance.upstream_generation_method: curated starter baseline with declared framings
provenance.review_method: Model-assisted structural review of the four supplied evidence notes and declared framing policies; no systematic full-text verification of the cited corpus

evidence.title: Egg production creates husbandry-dependent laying-hen welfare harms
evidence.claim: Whether eggs are “good to eat” can reasonably include the welfare of laying hens; welfare outcomes depend on housing and management and include risks such as bone lesions, restriction of behavior, group stress, and other husbandry-related harms.
evidence.evidence_quality: The EFSA opinion synthesizes peer-reviewed and grey literature, expert elicitation, and uncertainty analysis. It is authoritative for the questions it addresses, while also reporting limited evidence for some populations and uncertain relationships among group size, stocking density, and stress.
evidence.strongest_counterargument: Animal-welfare evidence does not itself determine whether eating eggs is morally acceptable. That conclusion also depends on ethical commitments, production system, geography, substitution effects, and how competing welfare outcomes are weighted.
evidence.confidence_assessment: No numeric confidence is assigned. The key epistemic point is that a population-health framing excludes this evidence by construction, not because the welfare considerations were assessed and found wanting.

citations.citation: EFSA Panel on Animal Health and Welfare (2023). Welfare of laying hens on farm. *EFSA Journal* 21(2):7789. https://doi.org/10.2903/j.efsa.2023.7789
European Food Safety Authority (2023). Plain-language summary: Welfare of laying hens. https://www.efsa.europa.eu/en/plain-language-summary/welfare-laying-hens

framing_assumptions: The welfare of laying hens is treated as part of whether consuming eggs is good, rather than as an external issue.
policy_sensitivities: Conclusions depend on production system, geography, husbandry practices, selected welfare outcomes, and the ethical weight assigned to animal harms.
coverage_gaps: The note does not identify the production system behind a particular egg purchase or convert welfare consequences into a settled moral verdict.
correlation_risks: The EFSA plain-language summary restates the scientific opinion and is not an independent source.
capability_limits: The supplied summary was reviewed without a systematic full-text audit of every cited source.

evidential: Evidence for some welfare consequences and system comparisons remains limited or uncertain.
ingestion_conditional: One European synthesis does not establish conditions across all jurisdictions and production systems.
capability_conditional: The system can describe reported harms but cannot resolve the normative weighting without declared values.
framing_conditional: A health-only source policy excludes these outcomes before their importance is considered.

notes: Keep descriptive welfare findings separate from the ethical conclusion drawn from them.
This is a substantive structural annotation of the supplied evidence note, not an independent finding that its factual claim is true.
